PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
WHAT IS THE SINGLE BEST WAY TO
BOOST SALES AF TER MY WEBINAR?
As you’ve learned in Webinar Funnel Formula ™, 50 to 70% of your sales can
happen outside of the webinar. To support those sales, you need a
conversion campaign. This is a post-webinar email campaign that you deliver
after the webinar has happened.
With the conversion campaign, you have a path for your prospects if they
attend the webinar (and if they buy, if they don't buy, etc). Then you have a
separate path for prospects if they don’t register for the webinar, so that you
can still continue to promote and sell to them.
(We’ll dive into the specifics of these email sequences further on in this
planner.)
Everything in the webinar sales funnel is based on whether or not you have
their email address. As you learned in Module 2, you have 3 potential
audiences (internal traffic, external traffic and affiliate traffic); the strategy for
converting each audience is different.
INTERNAL TRAFFIC SEQUENCE
This is where you already have their email address.
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	3 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
EXTERNAL TRAFFIC SEQUENCE
This is where you don’t have their email address.
AFFILIATE TRAFFIC SEQUENCE
This is where you don’t have their email address but your affiliate does.

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
KEY PROMOTIONAL TOOLS
In addition to your email sequences, there are some additional promotional
tools to use to support your post-webinar conversion campaign:
✓ 	Retargeting ads
✓ 	Personal videos
✓ 	Messenger/chat
✓ 	Social media
✓ 	Livestream video

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
WHAT IS THE WEBINAR REPL AY
PROMO?
For the approximately 70% who won’t sign up for a webinar, much less attend
one, it’s after the webinar is held that you need to kick into high
marketing mode. The question is where to introduce people who don’t sign
up to attend the webinar back into the webinar conversion funnel. The
answer is the Replay Promo.
THE REPLAY PROMO
The Replay Promo is 2 to 4 emails, inviting prospects to watch the replay of
your webinar.
Email #1. “Watch the Video”
✓ 	Sent: ASAP
✓ 	Focus: A Key aspect taught on the webinar
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	6 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 16 --

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
✓ 	Includes: What else they will learn from watching the replay (what you
marketed about it before)
Email #2. Timestamps from Webinar
✓ 	Sent: Next morning after webinar
✓ 	Focus: 3 key points from the webinar, including timestamps
✓ 	Includes: Testimonials from webinar attendees and webinar expiration
notification
Email #3. Bonus from the Webinar
✓ 	Sent: Afternoon of the day after webinar or the next morning
✓ 	Focus: “Download the Attendee Bonus from the webinar”
✓ 	Includes: Additional points from the webinar with timestamps and
webinar expiration notification. You could also include slide download.
(Optional… Replace email #3 with a Replay Report (see below))
Email #4.
✓ 	Sent: Day 3 after webinar
✓ 	Focus: Replay expiration
✓ 	Includes: Learning summary and Attendee Bonus
(Note: this email is sent after the Sales Page Promo begins)
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	7 	16
bonus tip!
If your webinar is converting less than 25%,
you may want to only send 2 of these
emails

-- 7 of 16 --

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
CUSTOMIZING YOUR REPLAY PROMO FOR AUDIENCE SEGMENTS
Remember, you are sending these emails to potentially four different
audiences, so you may want to address them differently in the text of your
emails.
Here’s how to customize the first email (“Watch the Video”) for each audience:
✓ 	Audience Segment 1. You want to communicate:
“Thanks for attending… Multiple people asked for the recording, so here it is.”
✓ 	Audience Segment 2. You want to communicate:
“Thanks for attending. I noticed you had to leave before the end, so here’s the
recording. Here are some things you may have missed…”
✓ 	Audience Segment 3. You want to communicate:
“Sorry you missed the webinar. Here’s what you missed… Here’s a copy of the
recording.”
✓ 	Audience Segment 4. You want to communicate:
“I recently recorded a webinar about ______________. Here’s what you missed…
Here’s a copy of the recording.”
THE REPLAY LANDING PAGE
The Replay Landing Page is the page that has the replay video on it. There are
several key elements to include on this page:
✓ 	Headline (similar to what you used on the webinar registration page)
✓ 	Replay Video (make sure it’s responsive/plays on mobile, and show the
video navigation bar)
✓ 	Countdown Timer (counting down to when the replay video disappears)
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	8 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 16 --

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
✓ 	Buy Button (have it appear at the time in the webinar when you’ve
revealed the price)
✓ 	Bonus download (link directly to the attendee bonus promised in your
Attendee Promo Sequence)
✓ 	If you use the Replay Report Strategy, have a download link to that
There are two alternative versions of the replay landing page:
✓ 	A version where the buy button appears immediately (for those who
previously watched the entire webinar)
✓ 	A version that requires an optin in order to view (to send cold traffic to via
advertising)
THE REPLAY REPORT STRATEGY
The Replay Report strategy is an alternative to the webinar replay video. It’s
a complete PDF report on the subject of your webinar.
The last section (where you make the offer), becomes more of a long-form
sales page but in print format, and ends with a buy button. Make sure to
clearly indicate your Cart Close date and use a tracking URL so you can
attribute those sales to the report.
Consider using the Replay Report strategy when:
✓ 	You’ve got a large audience
✓ 	Your content section teaches something step-by-step
✓ 	You’re working with less sophisticated audiences
✓ 	Your webinar isn’t converting as well as you want it to

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
HOW DO I CREATE AND PROMOTE MY
WEBINAR SALES PAGE?
Your webinar funnel sales page is where you’ll take orders. This page has to
work harder than a normal sales page, especially for those who don’t yet
know you very well.
CREATING YOUR WEBINAR SALES PAGE
There are two types of sales pages you’re going to use in your webinar
funnel; the webinar abbreviated sales page and the webinar funnel sales
page.
✓ 	Webinar Abbreviated Sales Page. This is the page you send people to
during the webinar. It’s short and designed to close the deal (you don’t
really need to do lots of selling on this page, as you’ve just done it in your
webinar).
✓ 	Webinar Funnel Sales Page. This is the page you send people to who
haven’t purchased from your previous promotions (webinar, replay). It’s
longer and more detailed (as this page has to make the sale and close the
deal).
WHAT TO INCLUDE ON YOUR WEBINAR SALES PAGE
There are two options for creating your webinar sales page (whether it’s
the webinar abbreviated sales page or the webinar funnel sales page):
✓ 	Sales Video Only Option
✓ 	Complete Sales Page Option
For more on this topic, see the
Webinar Sales Page Checklist
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	10 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 16 --

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
PROMOTING YOUR WEBINAR SALES PAGE
Once you’ve created your webinar sales page, you need a strategy to promote
it; that’s the Sales Page Promo.
SALES PAGE PROMO
The Sales Page Promo is where you do two things:
✓ 	Close the sale to those who have participated in the webinar funnel you’ve
created so far, but still haven’t purchased
✓ 	Appeal to those who won’t watch a webinar or a video
KEY COMMUNICATIONS
Remember, some of your prospects (less than 30%) have seen a webinar, and
some (about 20%), the replay, so the communications you make up until
your cart closes have to work extra hard.
Your mission is to get them to:
• Visit the sales page and absorb the information
• Re-visit the sales page if they went before and didn’t buy
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	11 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 16 --

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
Here’s what you need to communicate:
✓ 	“Here’s this cool product, you can buy it now” (remember, all previous
communications have talked about the webinar or replay, not the product
itself), so you need to specifically introduce the product and its benefits
✓ 	Proof (testimonials, case studies)
✓ 	Answers to key questions (you could also offer a Q&A webinar (see below)
or office hours so they can ask questions directly)
✓ 	Cart is closing (press the urgency and that they need to make a decision
now)

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
WHAT ARE SOME ADDITIONAL WAYS TO
GENERATE SALES POST-WEBINAR?
There are 3 additional strategies to consider for generating sales post-
webinar:
✓ 	The Q&A Sales Webinar
✓ 	Adding last minute bonuses
✓ 	Using an Abandoned-Cart Strategy
Q&A SALES WEBINAR
You just did a webinar, but it can help to have another webinar where you just
answer questions. This is the Q&A Sales Webinar. If you do this, you want to do
it the day before your cart closes. Your job on this webinar is to sell. You want
to communicate:
“I’m going to teach you __________. It’s way cool, you’ll definitely want to hear
this for about 15 minutes. Then I’m going to spend the rest of the time
answering your questions about (your product’s name) and (what your
product helps them do).”
On this webinar, you can be more aggressive in recommending your product
to people. You want to communicate:
“What’s holding you back? What’s going to change if you don’t invest? We
don’t push people to buy; you’ve got to do what’s right for you. But I will tell
you that…”
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	13 	16
bonus tip!
Your Q&A Sales Webinar is a great place to
introduce an extra bonus
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 16 --

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
ADDING LAST MINUTE BONUSES
One of the best things you can do during the Cart Open period is to add one
or more additional bonus. Your criteria for last minute bonuses:
✓ 	Something that they really want! (the more desirable it is, the more
success you’ll have with it)
✓ 	Something non essential, but so good they can’t resist such as:
✓ 	Extra time with you
✓ 	Something done for them
✓ 	Access to a killer program/tool for free
Keep in mind…
✓ 	You want to make it clear that everyone who has already purchased gets
this too
✓ 	This is not an “overcoming objection” bonus
ABANDONED-CART STRATEGY
You should definitely consider an Abandoned-Cart Strategy. 5-15% of people
who have abandoned carts end up completing their purchase with a good
abandoned cart strategy. This is especially valuable with higher-dollar sales.
✓ 	Set up a series of emails inviting them to complete their purchase. You
want to communicate:
“Did you forget something?… There’s still time… Last chance.”

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
WHEN AND HOW DO I CLOSE MY CART ?
You’ve set a deadline for your prospects to take advantage of your offer
(people tend to buy when they have a deadline). This is your Close Cart. This is
where you turn off your offer at a certain time on a certain date.
THE BEST DAY AND TIME TO CLOSE YOUR CART
The best days to close your cart are:
✓ 	Sunday
✓ 	Friday
✓ 	Wednesday
The best time of day to close your cart is midnight.
HOW TO CLOSE YOUR CART
This is how you close your cart:
✓ 	Do it by hand (you manually remove the offer from your sales page) or do
it via automated technology (such as Deadline Funnel)
✓ 	Replace your sales page/offer with something else
✓ 	A less special offer
✓ 	A waiting list page for the next time the program is available
HOW LONG TO LEAVE YOUR CART OPEN
✓ 	You should leave your cart open for 3 to 7 days; 5 days is ideal
YOUR POST-CLOSE OFFER
There are 2 options for creating a post-close offer for your prospects (those
who still haven’t bought via your webinar funnel):
Option 1
✓ 	Reopen your cart a few days after you close it (so long as you have logical
reason (holiday, weekend, technical issues, etc.)). This can bring in 5-15%
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	15 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 16 --

PLANNER / M7. POST-WEBINAR SALES PLANNER
more sales. You can also add additional extended payment options (6
months instead of 3).
Option 2
✓ 	Offer something of a lesser cost that helps move them towards the
transformation such as:
✓ 	The same program without the group coaching
✓ 	A lesser program with a different format
✓ 	A starter program
✓ 	Ask yourself, “What could I sell to someone who really wanted to buy but
truly couldn’t afford it?”
✓ 	Then ask, "When is the right time to make that offer to
them?” (sometimes it’s immediately after they decided not to buy,
sometimes it’s later)
YOUR POST-CLOSE STRATEGY
For anyone who has taken advantaged of your offer, keep in mind that you’ve
just generated a ton of good will, so use it:
✓ 	Give them some additional free content (such as surprise bonuses)
✓ 	Put them into a campaign to sell them something else in a couple of
weeks (it doesn’t have to be your product)
